description: Behavioral archetypes of clipping/aggregator social accounts. Each archetype defines a distinct content-selection logic and retention mechanism, derived from creator-economy research.
identity_constraint_axis: Archetypes range from tightly-constrained (one subject, one emotional register, one credibility posture) to unconstrained (opportunistic, taste-only). Tighter constraint correlates with predictable selection_logic and a retention_mechanism native to the content type itself. Looser constraint means retention depends almost entirely on production_signature execution.

types.label: Single-source, briefed distribution

examples.name: Gaming/stream clip campaigns (e.g. marketplace briefs built around a single streamer)
examples.descriptor: Twitch/gaming clips distributed via paid campaign briefs

selection_logic.rationale: Selection isn't made by the account operator; a client defines footage, narrative angle, and guidelines up front.

retention_mechanism.description: No independent account identity to build loyalty around; relies entirely on generic short-form execution (hook, pacing, captions).

classification_signals: Nearly all clips trace to a single named creator, show, or brand
Captions/bio reference sponsorship or campaign partnership
Consistent promotional CTA pointing back to the source

types.label: Single-subject fan/tribute account

examples.name: David Goggins Motivations
examples.descriptor: Instagram account branded as top fan page for one individual

selection_logic.rule: Subject presence is the only filter
selection_logic.rationale: Any appearance by the one named individual qualifies, regardless of source or topic.

retention_mechanism.description: Viewers return for a stable relationship with one recognizable person, not for any specific clip's content.

classification_signals: 100% of content features one named individual across many unrelated original sources
Account name/handle/bio explicitly names that individual
No consistent topic beyond the subject's presence

types.label: Multi-source vibe/emotion compilation

examples.name: Motiversity
examples.descriptor: Combines unrelated speakers (Goggins, Peterson, Eric Thomas, etc.) under cinematic scoring

selection_logic.rule: Does the moment deliver the target emotion
selection_logic.rationale: Subject and source are irrelevant; emotional register (e.g. grind/discipline catharsis) is the only filter.

retention_mechanism.description: Cinematic scoring and edit structure build a narrative arc across unrelated source material toward a climax.

classification_signals: Many different, unrelated named speakers with no single subject in common
Consistent non-diegetic music score, often cinematic/orchestral
Longer-than-typical runtime, structured as a build rather than a single punchline

types.label: Structural cliffhanger / advice

examples.name: The Girl's Bathroom podcast clips
examples.descriptor: Relationship-advice clips that cut before the resolution

selection_logic.rule: Stories with a clear dilemma that can be cut mid-story
selection_logic.rationale: The moment needs a natural incompleteness point to exploit.

retention_mechanism.description: The clip is deliberately incomplete; retention is measured off-platform (podcast listens), not within the clip.

classification_signals: Clip ends before a stated question, story, or dilemma resolves
Caption/on-screen text directs viewers elsewhere for the resolution
Source content is advice/relationship/dilemma-format audio

types.label: Edutainment / niche knowledge

selection_logic.rationale: Content must be interesting on factual merit, not just emotional charge.

retention_mechanism.description: Visual production (animation, graphics) compensates for what pure audio can't show, while the underlying insight still has to earn attention.

classification_signals: Content centers on a specific factual claim, analysis, or teachable insight
Heavy use of on-screen graphics/animation beyond basic captions
Consistent single subject-matter domain across posts

types.label: Personality banter

examples.name: Round Table (William Hanson & Jordan North)
examples.descriptor: Banter/entertainment clips from the hosts' own podcast

selection_logic.rationale: Low information density is acceptable; chemistry is the content.

retention_mechanism.description: Retention is a function of liking the people on screen, largely independent of topic.

classification_signals: Low information density per clip
Same 2+ recurring people across most/all posts
Captions/comments reference the hosts as personalities, not the topic

types.label: Manufactured controversy / debate

examples.name: First Take-style sports debate clips (e.g. Skip Bayless / Stephen A. Smith)
examples.descriptor: Hot-take and rivalry moments, not game footage

selection_logic.rule: The most inflammatory or rivalry-coded statement in the source
selection_logic.rationale: Explicitly avoids game highlights, which run into tighter league copyright enforcement.

retention_mechanism.description: Audience investment in a side/rivalry converts into comments and repeat viewing of the ongoing conflict.

classification_signals: Clipped moments center on strong opinions, insults, or disagreement between named figures
Absence of actual game/event footage despite a sports/competitive topic
Comment sections show visible 'sides' or camp-based argument

types.label: Credibility-first / restrained

examples.name: Health/longevity-science podcast clip campaigns
examples.descriptor: Briefs explicitly instruct 'reach and trust, not hype'

selection_logic.rule: Deliberately passes on the most sensational cut if it would misrepresent the source
selection_logic.rationale: Favors clear and accurate over shocking; sensationalism is a cost, not a benefit, in this category.

retention_mechanism.description: Slow-building trust relationship rather than single-clip virality.

classification_signals: Source domain is health, science, finance, or another claims-sensitive category
Selection favors clear explanation over the most dramatic possible moment
Bio/caption tone avoids hype language and superlatives

types.label: Gossip / drama aggregator

examples.name: Deuxmoi
examples.descriptor: Crowdsourced celebrity gossip from follower DMs

selection_logic.rule: Is it juicy and about someone recognizable
selection_logic.rationale: Platform or clip format barely matters; a large share of content is crowdsourced screenshots/DMs, not edited footage at all.

retention_mechanism.description: Retention comes from feeling 'in the know' plus ongoing storylines that reward regular check-ins.

classification_signals: Content mixes screenshots/text posts with video, not exclusively edited clips
Recurring subjects are public figures' interpersonal conflicts or controversies
Bio/posts reference audience tip submission as a content source

types.label: Opportunistic broad curator

examples.name: Generic 'find anything engaging' clip briefs
examples.descriptor: Explicitly instructed to pull funny, informative, controversial, or inspiring moments from any livestream, podcast, interview, or Twitter Spaces recording

selection_logic.rationale: The operator's own taste is the entire selection algorithm.

retention_mechanism.description: Nothing but production execution drives retention; there's no subject, vibe, or credibility posture to build a habit around.

classification_signals: Source content spans unrelated domains/topics/people with no consistent thread
Only consistent element across posts is the production treatment itself (hook style, caption font, zoom pattern)
No recurring named individual, brand, or topic across a sample of posts
